title: Teaching & Open Educational Resources
text: EUR_DATA is built around two modules at University College Dublin that integrate the study of EU politics with hands-on data analytics. The teaching materials below are released as open educational resources under CC-BY-4.0: you are free to reuse and adapt them with attribution.

title: Teaching & Open Educational Resources
text:
Modules

INRL20160 — The Politics of the EU (undergraduate)
An introduction to the institutions, actors, and policy processes of the European Union.



Year
Syllabus




2025-2026

PDF

POL40970 — The Politics of European Governance (postgraduate)
A graduate module on European governance, combining institutional analysis with quantitative methods and project-based work on real EU data.

title: Teaching & Open Educational Resources
section: Working with the data in teaching
text:
Working with the data in teaching
The Commission Formation dataset is designed for classroom use. The worked example notebook in the repository shows how to load and explore the tables: exploration.ipynb.

title: Data & Outputs
text: The EUR_DATA collection provides structured, analysis-ready datasets on European political institutions. Each dataset is released with reproducible pipeline code, a column-level codebook, and a test suite. All data is licensed CC-BY-4.0.

title: Data & Outputs
text:
Commission Formation (2024-2029)
The formation of the second von der Leyen Commission, from the June 2024 European Parliament elections to the adoption of the 2025 Commission Work Programme. Six interlinked tables, all keyed on a three-letter commissioner_id.








Table
Rows
What it captures




commissioners
27
The College: portfolio, party, gender, and DG assignments


mission_letter_commitments
721
Policy commitments from 26 presidential mission letters


hearings
26
Confirmation hearing dates, committees, and outcomes


investiture_vote
688
MEP-level roll-call votes on the College (27 Nov 2024)


work_programme_items
113
Items from the four annexes of the CWP 2025


formation_timeline
13
Key institutional milestones (250-day span)



Downloads: commissioners · commitments · hearings · investiture_vote · work_programme_items · formation_timeline
Every table is also available as XLSX in the same folder.
Documentation: Dataset README · Codebook






A citable DOI for this dataset is minted via Zenodo on each release. The DOI will appear here once the first release is published.




Explore the data
The charts below are interactive: hover for exact values.

voteData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/vote_by_group.csv").csv({typed: true})
commitData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/commitments_by_commissioner.csv").csv({typed: true})
annexData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/wp_by_annex.csv").csv({typed: true})
timelineData = FileAttachment("assets/charts/timeline.csv").csv({typed: true})






























Investiture vote by EP political group
The Parliament approved the College by 370 votes to 282, with 36 abstentions. The chart shows how each political group split.

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 40,
  x: {label: "MEPs", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: [...new Set(voteData.map(d => d.ep_party_group))]},
  color: {domain: ["for", "against", "abstain"], range: ["#2c7fb8", "#d73027", "#969696"], legend: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(voteData, {
      y: "ep_party_group",
      x: "count",
      fill: "vote",
      order: ["for", "against", "abstain"],
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})








Mission-letter commitments per commissioner
721 commitments were extracted from the 26 mission letters: 440 high-confidence (from bullet-point lists) and 281 medium-confidence (from directive sentences and legislative references).

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 150,
  marginBottom: 40,
  width: 800,
  height: 560,
  x: {label: "Commitments", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, sort: {y: "x", reduce: "sum", reverse: true}},
  color: {domain: ["high", "medium"], range: ["#1a9850", "#a6d96a"], legend: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(commitData, {
      y: "commissioner_name",
      x: "count",
      fill: "confidence",
      order: ["high", "medium"],
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})








Commission Work Programme 2025 by annex
113 items across the four annexes of the work programme adopted on 11 February 2025.

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 180,
  marginBottom: 40,
  x: {label: "Items", grid: true},
  y: {label: null, domain: annexData.map(d => d.label)},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(annexData, {y: "label", x: "count", fill: "#542788", tip: true}),
    Plot.text(annexData, {y: "label", x: "count", text: "count", dx: 12, fontWeight: "bold"}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})








Formation timeline
From the European Parliament elections (6 June 2024) to the adoption of the Commission Work Programme (11 February 2025).

Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 40,
  marginRight: 20,
  marginBottom: 50,
  height: 200,
  x: {label: null, type: "utc", grid: true},
  color: {legend: true, label: "Event type"},
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.dot(timelineData, {
      x: d => new Date(d.date),
      y: 0,
      fill: "event_type",
      r: 6,
      tip: {format: {x: true, y: false}},
      channels: {event: "event_name", institution: "institution"}
    })
  ]
})






More datasets will be added as the project develops.

title: About
text:
EUR_DATA (Integrating EU studies and Data Analytics using a Project-based Learning Approach) is an Erasmus+ Jean Monnet Module based at University College Dublin and maintained by the Connected Politics Lab. It is coordinated by James Cross.
The module integrates the study of European Union politics with hands-on data analytics. Students work with real institutional data to build practical quantitative skills, and the structured datasets they help produce are released here as open research resources.
This website hosts three kinds of output:

Data — open, analysis-ready datasets on European political institutions, each with a codebook, reproducible pipeline code, and tests.
Teaching — open educational resources, including module syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
Code — the full pipelines that build each dataset, on GitHub.

The module integrates the study of European Union politics with hands-on data analytics. Students work with real institutional data to build practical quantitative skills, and the structured datasets they help produce are released here as open research resources.
This website hosts three kinds of output:

Data — open, analysis-ready datasets on European political institutions, each with a codebook, reproducible pipeline code, and tests.
Teaching — open educational resources, including module syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
Code — the full pipelines that build each dataset, on GitHub.

title: About
text:
Licensing and citation
Data and educational resources are released under CC-BY-4.0; code under the Apache License 2.0. Please cite EUR_DATA if you use it; see the CITATION file.

title: About
text:
Contact
For questions, corrections, or feature requests, please open an issue on the GitHub repository.

title: About
text:

title: EUR_DATA
text:
EUR_DATA provides structured, analysis-ready datasets, reproducible code, and open educational resources on European political institutions. It is an Erasmus+ Jean Monnet Module based at University College Dublin and maintained by the Connected Politics Lab, integrating the study of EU politics with hands-on data analytics.



Commission Formation (2024-2029) — the formation of the second von der Leyen Commission: the College, mission-letter commitments, confirmation hearings, the investiture vote, the 2025 Work Programme, and a formation timeline. Six interlinked tables, 1,588 rows in total.

More datasets will be added as the project develops.




Data — dataset documentation, downloads, and interactive visualisations.
Teaching — open syllabi, slides, and assessment materials.
About — the project, its funding, and how to cite it.




Data and educational resources are released under CC-BY-4.0; code under the Apache License 2.0.
